title: Moisture-Shattering Thai Egg Omelette
description: A crispy roasted egg omelette, cooked to perfection with a delicate Thai twist.

ingredients.name: large eggs
ingredients.notes: room temperature eggs help the omelette cook evenly.

ingredients.name: unsweetened coconut milk
ingredients.notes: coconut milk helps keep the omelette moist and rich.

ingredients.name: chopped shallots
ingredients.notes: shallots add a sweet and savory flavor to the omelette.

ingredients.name: garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: garlic adds depth and aroma to the dish.

ingredients.name: fish sauce (or vegan alternative)
ingredients.notes: fish sauce adds umami flavor without overpowering the other ingredients.

ingredients.name: grated fresh ginger
ingredients.notes: ginger adds warmth and spice to the omelette.

ingredients.name: chopped cilantro
ingredients.notes: cilantro adds freshness and a pop of color.

ingredients.name: coconut oil
ingredients.notes: coconut oil helps the omelette cook evenly and adds a rich flavor.

instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together coconut milk, fish sauce, and grated ginger.
In a medium bowl, whisk together eggs, shallots, and garlic.
Heat 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium heat.
Pour in the egg mixture and cook until the edges start to set.
Pour the coconut milk mixture over the eggs and use a spatula to gently fold the edges towards the center.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and cook for 10-12 minutes, or until the omelette is set and slightly puffed.
Remove the skillet from the oven and garnish with chopped cilantro.
Use a spatula to carefully loosen the omelette from the skillet and transfer it to a plate.
Serve the moisture-shattering Thai egg omelette hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
